provenance.source_corpus: Re-establishment of hummock topography promotes tree regeneration on highly disturbed moderate-rich fens

source_spans.text: In this study, we examined the effectiveness of artificial mound formation to promote the natural regeneration of trees on highly disturbed fens.
conifer seedling density was more than five times higher on elevated spots than the mostly flat, flood-prone areas between them
Average density of conifer seedlings on mounds increased in a nearly linear pattern with average mound height
seedling density was positively related to mound height and strength of seed source
Such periodic flooding likely kills most conifer seedlings between elevated microsites
Such periodic flooding likely kills most conifer seedlings between elevated microsites
these pads had peat with higher humification
erosive forces were more damaging likely because the peat was thawing and fractured into smaller pieces during mound formation
operationally these pads were also treated in late winter when peat was thawing and fractured into pieces during mound construction
As most of the surfaces of these peat mounds were covered by mosses or Carex spp. by years four and five
erosion processes would be greatly slowed
